01-01.reflection: Mary, Mother of God, pray for us as we begin this new year. May her humble 'yes' inspire our own surrender to God's will.

01-06.reflection: St. Francis, help us see the divine in all of creation, especially in the poor and marginalized.

02-02.reflection: Presentation of the Lord: Like Francis, may we present our lives wholly to God in service and love.

02-11.reflection: Our Lady of Lourdes: St. Bernadette teaches us that humility and faith can lead to miraculous grace.

02-14.reflection: St. Valentine: True love is willing to sacrifice for the good of another, following Christ's example.

02-18.reflection: Feast of St. Bernadette: Her simple obedience at Lourdes shows how God works through the humble.

03-17.reflection: St. Patrick: May his courage in spreading the Gospel inspire us to share our faith boldly yet lovingly.

03-19.reflection: St. Joseph, guardian of the Holy Family, teach us quiet strength, responsible love, and faithful obedience.

03-25.reflection: Annunciation: Mary's 'fiat' invites us to say yes to God's plans, even when they surprise us.

04-04.reflection: St. Francis of Assisi: Peace prayer - Lord, make me an instrument of your peace...

04-25.reflection: St. Mark the Evangelist: May we have the courage to proclaim the Gospel in word and action.

05-01.reflection: St. Joseph the Worker: Honoring the dignity of human work and the call to labor for God's glory.

05-03.reflection: Saints Philip and James: Apostles who followed Christ to the ends of the earth.

05-13.reflection: Our Lady of Fatima: Call to prayer, penance, and conversion for the salvation of souls.

05-15.reflection: St. Isidore the Farmer: Holiness found in daily work and prayerful attention to God's presence.

06-24.reflection: St. John the Baptist: 'He must increase, but I must decrease' - a model of humility and mission.

06-29.reflection: Saints Peter and Paul: Pillars of the Church, differing in gifts but united in love for Christ.

07-03.reflection: St. Thomas the Apostle: From doubt to faith, his journey encourages our own search for the risen Lord.

07-25.reflection: St. James the Apostle: Zeal for the Gospel that led him to be the first martyr among the Twelve.

08-06.reflection: Feast of the Transfiguration: Christ's glory revealed, calling us to holiness and transformation.

08-08.reflection: St. Dominic: Founder of the Preachers, lover of truth and zeal for the salvation of souls.

08-14.reflection: St. Maximilian Kolbe: His act of love in Auschwitz shows that no act of charity is too great.

08-15.reflection: Assumption of Mary: Her heavenly birth reminds us of our eternal destiny with God.

08-27.reflection: St. Monica: Persistent prayer for her son Augustine teaches us never to give up hope in God's mercy.

09-08.reflection: Nativity of Mary: Her immaculate conception prepared her to be the pure Mother of God.

09-21.reflection: St. Matthew the Apostle: Former tax collector, shows that Christ calls everyone to repentance and new life.

09-23.reflection: St. Padre Pio: His stigmata and suffering united him closely with Christ's passion for the world.

10-01.reflection: St. Therese of Lisieux: 'Love is proved by deeds' - her little way shows holiness in small things.

10-04.reflection: St. Francis of Assisi: Peace prayer - Lord, make me an instrument of your peace...

10-12.reflection: Blessed Carlo Acutis: Used technology to spread Eucharistic miracles, showing holiness in the digital age.

10-15.reflection: St. Teresa of Avila: Mystic and reformer, teaches us deep prayer and courageous action for God's kingdom.

10-22.reflection: St. John Paul II: 'Be not afraid!' - his call to trust in God's mercy and engage the world with hope.

11-01.reflection: All Saints Day: We celebrate the communion of saints, both known and unknown, who now behold God face-to-face.

11-02.reflection: All Souls Day: We pray for the faithful departed, trusting in God's mercy and the hope of resurrection.

11-11.reflection: St. Martin of Tours: Known for sharing his cloak with a beggar, a model of compassionate action.

11-21.reflection: Presentation of Mary: Her lifelong dedication to God began with her consecration in the Temple.

11-22.reflection: St. Cecilia: Patroness of musicians, reminds us that all art can be used to glorify God.

11-25.reflection: St. Catherine of Alexandria: Courageous defender of the faith, even unto death.

12-03.reflection: St. Francis Xavier: Zealous missionary who brought the Gospel to Asia with relentless energy.

12-08.reflection: Immaculate Conception: Mary preserved from original sin, a testament to God's grace preparing her for Christ.

12-12.reflection: Our Lady of Guadalupe: Appeared to St. Juan Diego as a mestiza, showing God's love for all peoples.

12-13.reflection: St. Lucia: Martyr who held fast to her faith, a witness to the light of Christ in darkness.

12-25.reflection: Christmas Day: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us. Contemplate the humility of God becoming man.

12-26.reflection: St. Stephen, first martyr: Died praying for his persecutors, a model of forgiving love.

12-27.reflection: St. John the Apostle: The beloved disciple who rested his head on Jesus' chest at the Last Supper.

12-28.reflection: Holy Innocents: Remembering the children killed by Herod, a call to protect all human life.

12-31.reflection: St. Sylvester I: Pope during Constantine's era, reminds us to seek God's kingdom above all earthly powers.

03-16.reflection: St. Joseph, model of workers and fathers, teach us to honor our duties with patience and love, trusting in God's providence.
